data.description:
Goal:
Close the K-36 follow-up gap: the pending-signature directory
(`secrets/.pending-recipient-adds/`) is single-tenant — only one
recipient-add can be in flight per repo at any time. A team with
multiple concurrent adds across different namespaces (e.g. adding
alice to `web-api/staging` while bob is being added to
`worker/production`) can't run them in parallel. Shard the pending
directory by namespace so concurrent quorum-add flows don't collide.

Verification:
1. Pending signatures live at
   `secrets/.pending-recipient-adds/<app>/<env>/<thumbprint>-<recipient-sha>.sig`.
2. `thimble recipient sign-add <app> <env> ...` writes to the
   per-namespace path; `thimble recipient add` reads from the same.
3. Two concurrent quorum-add flows (web-api/staging and
   worker/production) each accumulate signatures independently and
   each succeeds at commit time without seeing the other's pending
   files.
4. Migration: any existing pending files at the old flat path are
   moved to the new sharded path on first use, with a one-line
   stderr note. After migration, the old flat path is empty and the
   loader stops looking there.
5. Test: integration test with two namespaces sees two separate
   pending dirs; cross-namespace pollution does not happen.

Constraints:
- Knot ref: K-51; follow-up to K-36 (thimble-f550).
- Files: internal/quorum/{prepare,sign,verify}.go, internal/store/quorum_sign.go, docs/recipient-quorum.md.
- Backwards compat: handle the migration cleanly so existing in-flight signatures aren't lost when this knot lands.
- Notes: this is purely a layout change, not a protocol change. The signature contents are unchanged; only the path resolution shifts.
